Title: Create RFQ from Cart
Preconditions: Logged in as B2B org buyer ({{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}); cart contains 2+ items from enabled catalog; RFQ feature enabled for the org
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to cart page; [ASSERT] verify 2+ line items present; [ACT] click 'Request Quote' button; [WAIT] wait for RFQ modal or page to load; [ASSERT] verify RFQ form pre-populates with cart line items and quantities; [ACT] fill specifications/notes field with free-text comment; [ACT] click 'Submit' on RFQ form; [WAIT] wait for confirmation response; [ASSERT] verify confirmation message shown; [NAV] navigate to /account/quotes; [ASSERT] verify new quote appears with status 'Processing'; [NAV] return to cart; [ASSERT] verify cart items still present after RFQ submission
Assertions: [DOM] 'Request Quote' button visible for B2B org user; [DOM] RFQ form displays each cart line item with name and quantity; [STATE] quote status = 'Processing' in /account/quotes list; [DOM] confirmation message contains 'submitted' or 'request received'; [STATE] cart item count unchanged after RFQ submission
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] POST createQuoteFromCart mutation — response errors[] is empty; [CONSOLE] no JS errors on RFQ form submission; [NETWORK] 2xx response on quote creation endpoint
Failure_Signals: 'Request Quote' button absent for org buyer; RFQ form fails to pre-populate cart items; quote does not appear in /account/quotes after submission
Cleanup: reject quote via admin if accepted/pending

Title: View Quote in Account
Preconditions: RFQ submitted and confirmed with status 'Processing'; logged in as same B2B org buyer who submitted the RFQ
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to account menu; [ACT] click 'Quotes' or navigate to /account/quotes; [WAIT] wait for quotes list to load; [ASSERT] verify submitted quote appears in list; [ASSERT] verify Quote ID displayed; [ASSERT] verify submission date shown; [ASSERT] verify status badge shows 'Processing'; [ASSERT] verify item count matches original cart
Assertions: [DOM] /account/quotes page loads without error; [DOM] quote row shows Quote ID; [DOM] submission date formatted correctly; [STATE] status badge text = 'Processing'; [DOM] item count on row matches number of cart items submitted
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] GET quotes query returns the quote record; [CONSOLE] no 404 or auth errors on quotes list page
Failure_Signals: quotes list page returns 404 or access denied; submitted quote not visible in list; status shows value other than 'Processing'

Title: Quote Detail Page
Preconditions: Quote exists in 'Processing' status in buyer's account; logged in as the B2B org buyer who created the quote
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/quotes; [ACT] click on the quote to open detail page; [WAIT] wait for detail page to load; [ASSERT] verify page title or header shows Quote ID; [ASSERT] verify each line item displays product name and requested quantity; [ASSERT] verify specifications/notes entered during RFQ submission are visible; [ASSERT] verify current status is displayed prominently; [ASSERT] verify submission date shown
Assertions: [DOM] quote detail page loads without error; [DOM] all line items from original RFQ shown with product name and qty; [DOM] specs/notes text matches text entered at submission; [STATE] status indicator shows 'Processing'; [FORMAT] Quote ID and submission date formatted consistently
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] GET quote detail query returns full line items; [CONSOLE] no JS errors on detail page load
Failure_Signals: detail page returns 404; line items missing or count incorrect; specs/notes field empty when text was entered; status indicator absent

Title: Admin Response Notification
Preconditions: Admin has reviewed and responded to buyer's quote with proposed pricing; buyer account is {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}; admin has access via {{ADMIN_URL}}
Steps: [NAV] Log in as B2B org buyer ({{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}); [ASSERT] check storefront header notification area for new alert badge; [ACT] click notification or navigate to /account/quotes; [WAIT] wait for quotes list to refresh; [ASSERT] verify quote that admin responded to shows updated status ('Quote Received' or equivalent); [ASSERT] verify notification or badge indicates admin has responded
Assertions: [DOM] notification badge or alert visible in header indicating quote update; [STATE] quote status changed from 'Processing' to 'Quote Received' or equivalent; [DOM] updated status reflected in quotes list without manual refresh
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] GET notifications or quote status query shows updated state; [CONSOLE] no errors loading notification data
Failure_Signals: no notification badge shown after admin response; quote still shows 'Processing' status after admin submission; no visual indicator of state change

Title: View Updated Quote Pricing
Preconditions: Admin has responded to buyer's quote with proposed line-item pricing; quote status is 'Quote Received'; buyer logged in as {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/quotes; [ACT] click on the quote with Admin's pricing response; [WAIT] wait for detail page to load; [ASSERT] verify Admin's proposed unit price shown per line item; [ASSERT] verify original requested/catalog price shown alongside for comparison; [ASSERT] verify total proposed quote value shown; [ASSERT] verify validity/expiry date displayed if set by Admin
Assertions: [DOM] each line item shows Admin-proposed unit price; [DOM] comparison price (original) visible per line; [MATH] total quote value = sum of (proposed unit price × quantity) for all lines; [FORMAT] expiry date formatted as readable date; [STATE] status shows 'Quote Received'
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] GET quote detail returns admin_price fields per line item; [CONSOLE] no rendering errors on quote detail with pricing
Failure_Signals: line items show no price values; Admin-proposed price missing; quote total not updated after admin response; expiry date absent when set

Title: Accept Quote
Preconditions: Quote with Admin pricing response is available; buyer logged in as {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}; quote status is 'Quote Received'
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/quotes; [ACT] click on quote with Admin pricing; [WAIT] wait for detail page; [ASSERT] verify 'Accept Quote' button is visible; [ACT] click 'Accept Quote'; [WAIT] wait for confirmation dialog; [ASSERT] verify confirmation dialog appears; [ACT] confirm acceptance in dialog; [WAIT] wait for status update; [ASSERT] verify quote status changes to 'Accepted'; [ASSERT] verify 'Convert to Order' button now visible; [ASSERT] verify 'Reject Quote' button no longer visible
Assertions: [DOM] 'Accept Quote' button present when quote has Admin pricing; [DOM] confirmation dialog displayed before committing; [STATE] status = 'Accepted' after confirmation; [DOM] 'Convert to Order' button visible after acceptance; [DOM] 'Reject Quote' button absent after acceptance; [STATE] acceptance timestamp recorded on detail page
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] POST acceptQuote mutation — response errors[] is empty; [CONSOLE] no errors on acceptance flow; [ADMIN] verify quote status updated to Accepted in Admin portal
Failure_Signals: 'Accept Quote' button absent when Admin pricing present; dialog does not appear before acceptance; status remains 'Quote Received' after confirmation; 'Convert to Order' button absent after acceptance
Cleanup: reject quote via admin if accepted/pending

Title: Convert Quote to Order
Preconditions: Quote status is 'Accepted'; 'Convert to Order' button visible on quote detail; buyer logged in as {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}; payment method configured
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/quotes; [ACT] click on accepted quote; [WAIT] wait for detail page; [ACT] click 'Convert to Order'; [WAIT] wait for checkout flow to load; [ASSERT] verify checkout pre-populated with quote line items and quantities; [ASSERT] verify prices displayed are Admin-quoted prices not current catalog prices; [ACT] select shipping address; [ACT] select shipping method; [ACT] confirm payment method; [ACT] click 'Place Order'; [WAIT] wait for order confirmation page; [ASSERT] verify order confirmation shows quoted prices; [NAV] navigate to /account/quotes; [ASSERT] verify quote status is 'Ordered' or 'Closed'
Assertions: [DOM] checkout pre-populated with all accepted quote line items; [MATH] checkout line prices = Admin-quoted prices not catalog prices; [STATE] quote status = 'Ordered' or 'Closed' after conversion; [DOM] order confirmation references original Quote ID; [NAV] redirect to order confirmation page after place order
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] POST createOrderFromCart mutation — response errors[] is empty; [API] verify order total in response matches sum of (quoted unit price × qty) for all lines; [CONSOLE] no errors during checkout from quote; [NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on order placement endpoint
Failure_Signals: checkout opens with catalog prices instead of quoted prices; 'Convert to Order' fails or redirects to error page; order confirmation total does not match quoted total; quote status remains 'Accepted' after order placement
Cleanup: none — order placed is final; verify quote status updated to Ordered/Closed

Title: Reject Quote
Preconditions: Quote with Admin pricing response is available; buyer logged in as {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}; quote status is 'Quote Received'
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/quotes; [ACT] click on quote with Admin pricing; [WAIT] wait for detail page; [ASSERT] verify 'Reject Quote' button visible; [ACT] click 'Reject Quote'; [WAIT] wait for rejection reason dialog or form; [ASSERT] verify rejection reason text field displayed; [ACT] enter rejection reason text; [ACT] submit rejection; [WAIT] wait for status update; [ASSERT] verify status changes to 'Rejected'; [ASSERT] verify rejection reason visible in quote history or detail; [ADMIN] in Admin portal navigate to the quote; [ASSERT] verify admin sees the rejection and reason
Assertions: [DOM] 'Reject Quote' button visible when Admin pricing present; [DOM] rejection reason field is present and required (cannot submit empty); [STATE] quote status = 'Rejected' after submission; [DOM] rejection reason text visible on quote detail; [DOM] 'Convert to Order' button absent on rejected quote; [ADMIN] Admin portal shows Rejected status and buyer's reason
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] POST rejectQuote mutation — response errors[] is empty; [ADMIN] quote status in Admin portal = Rejected; [CONSOLE] no errors on rejection flow
Failure_Signals: rejection reason field accepts submission without text; status does not change to 'Rejected'; 'Convert to Order' still visible after rejection; rejection reason not shown in quote history

Title: Quote Negotiation
Preconditions: Quote exists with Admin's initial pricing response; buyer logged in as {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}; quote in 'Quote Received' status
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/quotes; [ACT] click on quote with Admin pricing; [WAIT] wait for detail page; [ASSERT] verify 'Request Revision' or 'Counter-offer' option visible; [ACT] click revision option; [ACT] enter desired price adjustment or comment in provided field; [ACT] submit revision request; [WAIT] wait for status update; [ASSERT] verify quote status reflects pending negotiation state; [KEY] admin logs in at {{ADMIN_URL}} and updates pricing; [NAV] return to storefront as buyer; [NAV] navigate to /account/quotes; [ACT] click the quote; [ASSERT] verify revised pricing visible; [ASSERT] verify negotiation history or comments trail shown
Assertions: [DOM] revision or counter-offer option visible on quote detail; [DOM] comment/price field accepts buyer input; [STATE] quote status transitions to negotiation/pending-revision state after submission; [DOM] revised Admin pricing displayed after admin update; [DOM] negotiation history or comment thread visible on quote
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] quote status update request returns 2xx; [CONSOLE] no errors submitting revision request
Failure_Signals: revision option absent on quote detail; revision submission fails silently; quote status not updated after revision request; negotiation history not shown

Title: Quote with Substitution
Preconditions: Admin has offered an alternate substitute product on at least one quote line; buyer logged in as {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/quotes; [ACT] click on quote where admin added a substitute; [WAIT] wait for detail page; [ASSERT] verify affected line item has a 'Substitution' badge or label; [ASSERT] verify both original product and substitute product shown on the line; [ASSERT] verify substitute shows product name, SKU, unit price, and availability; [ACT] click accept on the substitution (if accept/reject per-line option available); [ASSERT] verify accepting updates that line item for order conversion
Assertions: [DOM] substituted line flagged with 'Substitution' badge or equivalent indicator; [DOM] both original and substitute product details visible on the line; [DOM] substitute SKU, name, price, and availability all present; [STATE] accepting substitution updates line item in quote; [DOM] rejected substitution reverts line to original or marks as rejected
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] quote detail response includes substitution data per affected line; [CONSOLE] no rendering errors displaying dual-product line items
Failure_Signals: substitution badge absent on affected line; substitute product details missing (no SKU or price); accept/reject action on substitution does not update line state

Title: Quote Expiry
Preconditions: Quote with an expiry date set by Admin; the expiry date has passed; buyer logged in as {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/quotes; [ASSERT] locate quote past its expiry date; [ASSERT] verify quote shows 'Expired' status badge in list; [ACT] click on the expired quote; [WAIT] wait for detail page; [ASSERT] verify expiry date displayed on detail; [ASSERT] verify 'Convert to Order' button is NOT present or is disabled; [ASSERT] verify 'Accept Quote' button is NOT present or is disabled; [ASSERT] verify option to submit new RFQ for same items is present
Assertions: [DOM] expired quote shows 'Expired' status badge in list; [DOM] expiry date shown on detail page; [DOM] 'Convert to Order' button absent or visually disabled with no active click handler; [DOM] 'Accept Quote' button absent or disabled; [DOM] new RFQ option available for expired quote
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] GET quote detail returns expiry_date field; [CONSOLE] no errors accessing expired quote detail
Failure_Signals: expired quote shows no status change; 'Convert to Order' remains clickable on expired quote; 'Accept Quote' remains active; expiry date absent from detail page

Title: Quote History Filter
Preconditions: Multiple quotes exist with statuses: Processing; Accepted; Rejected; Expired; buyer logged in as {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/quotes; [ASSERT] verify filter control (dropdown or tabs) visible; [ACT] select filter 'Processing'; [ASSERT] verify only Processing quotes shown; [ACT] select filter 'Accepted'; [ASSERT] verify only Accepted quotes shown; [ACT] select filter 'Rejected'; [ASSERT] verify only Rejected quotes shown; [ACT] select filter 'Expired'; [ASSERT] verify only Expired quotes shown; [ACT] clear filter; [ASSERT] verify all quotes shown again
Assertions: [DOM] filter control visible on quotes list page; [STATE] each status filter shows only quotes matching that status; [DOM] quote count updates after each filter change; [STATE] clearing filter restores full list; [DOM] active filter state visually indicated (highlighted tab or selected dropdown value)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] filtered quote list queries return correctly scoped results; [CONSOLE] no errors on filter state changes
Failure_Signals: filter control absent on quotes list; filtering shows quotes of incorrect statuses; count does not update after filter; page error on filter selection

Title: Quote with Attachments
Preconditions: User is logged in as B2B org buyer ({{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}); cart contains items; RFQ feature enabled; file attachment feature enabled on RFQ form
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to cart page; [ACT] click 'Request Quote'; [WAIT] wait for RFQ form to load; [ASSERT] verify file attachment field present on form; [ASSERT] verify accepted file types and size limit shown; [ACT] upload a PDF or image file via attachment field; [WAIT] wait for upload to complete; [ASSERT] verify filename displayed on form with remove option; [ACT] submit RFQ; [WAIT] wait for confirmation; [NAV] navigate to /account/quotes; [ACT] click the newly created quote; [ASSERT] verify attachment visible on quote detail; [KEY] verify admin can download attachment from Admin portal
Assertions: [DOM] file upload field present on RFQ form; [DOM] accepted types and size limit shown; [DOM] uploaded filename displayed with remove button before submission; [DOM] attachment listed on quote detail page after submission; [ADMIN] attachment downloadable from Admin portal quote detail
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] POST createQuoteFromCart mutation — response errors[] is empty; [NETWORK] file upload request returns 2xx; [CONSOLE] no upload errors
Failure_Signals: file upload field absent on RFQ form; upload fails for valid file types; filename not shown after upload; attachment absent on quote detail after submission
Cleanup: reject quote via admin if accepted/pending

Title: Multiple Active Quotes
Preconditions: At least 3 quotes exist with different statuses; buyer logged in as {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/quotes; [ASSERT] verify all quotes listed in list/table format; [ASSERT] verify each row shows Quote ID; [ASSERT] verify each row shows submission date; [ASSERT] verify each row shows status badge; [ASSERT] verify each row shows item count; [ASSERT] verify default sort is most recent first; [ACT] click date column header; [ASSERT] verify sort order reverses; [ACT] click status column header (if available); [ASSERT] verify quotes grouped or sorted by status
Assertions: [DOM] quotes list/table shows all existing quotes; [DOM] each row contains Quote ID, date, status, and item count; [FORMAT] submission dates formatted consistently; [STATE] default sort = most recent first (descending date); [DOM] column sort headers functional; [DOM] pagination or infinite scroll present if quote count exceeds page size
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] GET quotes list returns all quotes with full metadata; [CONSOLE] no errors on sort/pagination interaction
Failure_Signals: quotes list empty when quotes exist; rows missing Quote ID or status; default sort is not most recent first; column sort action causes page error

Title: RFQ from Saved List
Preconditions: User logged in as {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}; saved list (My Lists) exists with products; RFQ feature enabled; cart may be empty (test must work independently of cart)
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/lists; [ACT] open a saved list containing products; [WAIT] wait for list detail page to load; [ASSERT] verify 'Request Quote' button visible on list page; [ACT] click 'Request Quote' from list; [WAIT] wait for RFQ form to load; [ASSERT] verify RFQ form pre-populates with all list items and quantities; [ACT] fill specifications field; [ACT] submit RFQ; [WAIT] wait for confirmation; [NAV] navigate to /account/quotes; [ASSERT] verify new quote appears with status 'Processing'; [NAV] return to /account/lists; [ASSERT] verify list items still present after RFQ submission
Assertions: [DOM] 'Request Quote' button visible on saved list detail page; [DOM] RFQ form pre-populates with all list items independently of cart state; [STATE] submitted quote appears in /account/quotes with status 'Processing'; [STATE] saved list item count unchanged after RFQ submission
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] POST createQuoteFromCart or createQuoteFromList mutation — response errors[] is empty; [CONSOLE] no errors on RFQ submission from list context
Failure_Signals: 'Request Quote' button absent on saved list page; RFQ form uses cart items instead of list items; list items removed after RFQ submission; quote not created in /account/quotes
Cleanup: reject quote via admin if accepted/pending

Title: Reorder - All Items Added to Cart at Current Prices
Preconditions: Logged in as buyer ({{USER_EMAIL}}) with at least one completed order in history; all ordered items still available in catalog at current prices
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/orders; [ASSERT] locate a completed order with multiple items; [KEY] note original item prices and quantities from order detail; [ACT] click 'Reorder' on the completed order; [WAIT] wait for cart page to load; [ASSERT] verify all original items now in cart; [ASSERT] verify quantities match original order quantities; [ASSERT] verify prices reflect current catalog prices not historical order prices; [ASSERT] verify success notification confirms items added
Assertions: [DOM] all items from original order present in cart after reorder; [MATH] quantities in cart = original order quantities for each line item; [STATE] cart prices = current catalog prices (verify at least one price matches live catalog); [DOM] success notification visible after reorder action; [DOM] cart subtotal calculated using current prices
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] reorder mutation response contains all expected line items; [CONSOLE] no errors on cart page after reorder; [NETWORK] 2xx on add-to-cart requests
Failure_Signals: not all items added to cart after reorder; quantities defaulted to 1 instead of original values; historical order prices used instead of current catalog prices

Title: Reorder - Discontinued Item Handled Gracefully
Preconditions: Logged in as buyer ({{USER_EMAIL}}) with a completed order containing at least one discontinued/unavailable product and at least one currently available product
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/orders; [ASSERT] locate order containing discontinued product; [ACT] click 'Reorder'; [WAIT] wait for cart page to load; [ASSERT] verify available items added to cart; [ASSERT] verify discontinued item NOT present in cart; [ASSERT] verify warning message identifies which item(s) could not be added; [ASSERT] verify warning specifies reason (e.g. product no longer available); [ASSERT] verify cart is functional with available items only
Assertions: [DOM] available items present in cart after reorder; [STATE] discontinued item absent from cart line items; [DOM] warning/notification message lists the unavailable item by name or SKU; [DOM] cart proceed-to-checkout remains functional with available items; [DOM] user not blocked from continuing with partial cart
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] reorder mutation response includes unavailable_items or partial_result field; [CONSOLE] no unhandled JS exceptions on cart page with partial reorder; [NETWORK] check for any 404s on discontinued product catalog endpoints
Failure_Signals: reorder silently drops all items including available ones; no warning shown for discontinued item; discontinued item added to cart causing downstream errors; cart blocked for all items due to one unavailable item

Title: Reorder - Quantities Match Original Order
Preconditions: Logged in as buyer ({{USER_EMAIL}}) with a completed order containing items with varied quantities (e.g. Product A qty 2; Product B qty 5)
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/orders; [ACT] click on target order to open detail; [KEY] record each line item SKU and quantity (e.g. Product A = 2; Product B = 5); [ACT] click 'Reorder'; [WAIT] wait for cart page; [ASSERT] verify Product A quantity in cart = 2; [ASSERT] verify Product B quantity in cart = 5; [ASSERT] verify no item defaulted to quantity 1 instead of original value; [MATH] verify cart subtotal = sum of (current unit price × original quantity) for all lines
Assertions: [DOM] each reordered item present in cart; [MATH] qty per cart line = original order qty for that SKU; [MATH] cart subtotal reflects correct quantities multiplied by current prices; [STATE] no cart line item shows qty = 1 when original order qty was greater than 1
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] reorder mutation response line items include correct quantity values; [CONSOLE] no errors processing quantity mapping in cart
Failure_Signals: any item quantity defaults to 1 instead of original order quantity; item count in cart differs from original order line count; cart subtotal inconsistent with quantities × current prices

Title: Order Status - New Order Shows Correct Status
Preconditions: Buyer logged in as {{USER_EMAIL}}; able to complete checkout and place a new order; payment method available
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to storefront and add item to cart; [ACT] proceed through checkout; [ACT] place order; [WAIT] wait for order confirmation page; [KEY] note order number from confirmation; [NAV] navigate to /account/orders; [ASSERT] find newly placed order in list; [ACT] click order to view detail; [ASSERT] verify order status = 'New'; [ASSERT] verify order date = today's date; [ASSERT] verify all order details complete (items, quantities, prices, shipping address)
Assertions: [STATE] order status = 'New' immediately after placement; [DOM] order visible in /account/orders list; [FORMAT] order date matches current date ({{currentDate}} format); [DOM] order detail page shows all items, quantities, shipping, and payment summary; [DOM] order number on detail matches confirmation page
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] GET orders list includes new order with status New; [CONSOLE] no errors loading order history after placement
Failure_Signals: newly placed order absent from /account/orders list; status shows incorrect value (e.g. Processing before admin action); order date missing or incorrect; order detail missing line items
Cleanup: none — order is read-only after placement

Title: Order Status - Admin Ships Order With Tracking
Preconditions: Order exists in 'New' status placed by {{USER_EMAIL}}; admin credentials {{ADMIN_EMAIL}} / {{ADMIN_PASSWORD}} available; admin can access {{ADMIN_URL}}
Steps: [ADMIN] Log in to Admin SPA at {{ADMIN_URL}}; [NAV] navigate to Orders; [ACT] find target order by order number; [ACT] update order status to 'Shipped'; [ACT] enter tracking number '1Z999AA10123456784' in tracking field; [ACT] save changes; [ASSERT] verify admin save succeeded (no error messages); [NAV] return to storefront at {{FRONT_URL}} and log in as {{USER_EMAIL}}; [NAV] navigate to /account/orders; [ASSERT] find order in list showing 'Shipped' status; [ACT] click order to view detail; [ASSERT] verify status = 'Shipped' on detail page; [ASSERT] verify tracking number '1Z999AA10123456784' displayed; [ASSERT] verify tracking number is a clickable link
Assertions: [ADMIN] admin save completes without error; [STATE] order status = 'Shipped' in storefront list and detail after admin update; [DOM] tracking number '1Z999AA10123456784' visible on order detail; [DOM] tracking number rendered as clickable link; [DOM] status history may show New → Shipped transition
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ADMIN] verify admin Orders list shows Shipped status for the order; [API] GET order detail returns status=Shipped and tracking_number field; [CONSOLE] no errors on storefront order detail page after admin status update
Failure_Signals: admin save fails silently and status not updated; storefront still shows 'New' after admin ships; tracking number absent from order detail; tracking number displayed as plain text not a link
Cleanup: none — status update is intentional test action

Title: Order Status Filter - Filter by Shipped
Preconditions: Buyer logged in as {{USER_EMAIL}}; account has multiple orders in different statuses including at least one 'Shipped' and at least one in another status
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/orders; [ASSERT] verify status filter dropdown or control visible; [ACT] select 'Shipped' from status filter; [WAIT] wait for filtered results to load; [ASSERT] verify all displayed orders have status 'Shipped'; [ASSERT] verify no orders with other statuses appear; [ASSERT] verify order count updates to reflect filtered set; [ACT] clear or reset filter; [WAIT] wait for full list to reload; [ASSERT] verify all orders shown again with no active filter
Assertions: [DOM] status filter control present on /account/orders page; [STATE] all visible orders show 'Shipped' status after filter applied; [STATE] orders with New/Processing/Completed statuses absent from filtered view; [DOM] order count updates after filter; [STATE] clearing filter restores full order list; [DOM] no page errors or blank state after filter interaction
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] filtered orders query scoped to status=Shipped; [CONSOLE] no JS errors on filter interaction; [NETWORK] 2xx on filtered orders request
Failure_Signals: status filter control absent from orders page; filter shows orders with non-Shipped statuses; clearing filter does not restore full list; page throws error on filter selection

Title: Reorder into Non-Empty Cart - Items Merge
Preconditions: Buyer logged in as {{USER_EMAIL}}; cart already contains at least one item (Item A, qty 1); at least one completed order exists in /account/orders; the completed order contains Item A (same SKU as cart item) plus at least one other item
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to a product page for {{TEST_SKU}} and add qty 1 to cart; [WAIT] wait for cart badge to update; [KEY] note Item A SKU and current cart quantity = 1; [NAV] navigate to /account/orders; [ACT] locate completed order containing Item A and at least one other item; [ACT] click 'Reorder' on that order; [WAIT] wait for cart page to load; [ASSERT] verify Item A quantity in cart = original order qty + 1 (merged, not duplicate line); [ASSERT] verify no duplicate line items exist for Item A SKU; [ASSERT] verify other order items also present in cart; [ASSERT] verify success notification shown
Assertions: [DOM] cart contains exactly one line per SKU — no duplicate rows for Item A; [MATH] Item A qty in cart = pre-existing qty (1) + reorder qty from completed order; [DOM] all other reordered items present as separate line items; [STATE] cart item count reflects merged total not additive count; [DOM] success or reorder confirmation message visible
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] reorder mutation response — verify no duplicate SKU entries in returned cart lines; [CONSOLE] no JS errors on cart page after merge; [NETWORK] 2xx on reorder request
Failure_Signals: Item A appears as two separate line items in cart (duplication bug); Item A quantity shows only the reorder quantity ignoring pre-existing qty; cart cleared before reorder items added; success notification absent

Title: Reorder into Cart with Items from Same Org - Merge Behavior Verified
Preconditions: Buyer logged in as {{USER_EMAIL}}; cart already contains items from the same store/org; completed order exists from same store/org; some SKUs overlap between cart and order
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to storefront and add 2 distinct products to cart (Product X qty 2, Product Y qty 1); [KEY] note SKUs and quantities; [NAV] navigate to /account/orders; [ACT] locate a completed order containing Product X (same SKU) and Product Z (not in cart); [ACT] click 'Reorder'; [WAIT] wait for cart page to load; [ASSERT] verify Product X quantity incremented (not duplicated) to 2 + original order qty for X; [ASSERT] verify Product Y still present with qty 1 unchanged; [ASSERT] verify Product Z added from reorder; [ASSERT] verify cart total line count = 3 (X, Y, Z) not 4
Assertions: [DOM] exactly 3 cart line items — no duplicate rows; [MATH] Product X qty = cart qty (2) + reorder qty; [STATE] Product Y qty unchanged at 1; [DOM] Product Z present from reorder; [MATH] cart subtotal = sum of all 3 line items at current catalog prices; [STATE] no items lost from pre-existing cart during reorder merge
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] cart query after reorder returns 3 distinct line items; [CONSOLE] no merge errors or warnings in console; [NETWORK] no 4xx on cart update requests
Failure_Signals: pre-existing cart items lost when reorder executes; Product X duplicated as two rows; Product Y quantity altered by reorder; cart total incorrect after merge

Title: Return Request - Submit with Reason
Preconditions: Buyer logged in as {{USER_EMAIL}}; at least one completed/delivered order exists in /account/orders; return/RMA feature enabled for the store
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/orders; [ACT] click on a completed order to open detail page; [ASSERT] verify 'Return' or 'Request Return' button visible on order detail; [ACT] click 'Return' / 'Request Return'; [WAIT] wait for return form or modal to load; [ASSERT] verify return form lists all order line items with checkboxes; [ACT] select all items to return; [ACT] select or enter return reason (e.g. 'Defective product'); [ACT] click submit/confirm return request; [WAIT] wait for confirmation response; [ASSERT] verify confirmation message or success notification shown; [ASSERT] verify return request status visible on order detail page
Assertions: [DOM] 'Return' or 'Request Return' button present on completed order detail; [DOM] return form lists all order line items with selection mechanism; [DOM] return reason field present and required; [STATE] confirmation message shown after successful submission; [STATE] return request status or badge visible on order detail after submission; [DOM] return reference number or ID shown in confirmation
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] POST return/RMA mutation — response errors[] is empty; [CONSOLE] no JS errors on return form submission; [NETWORK] 2xx on return request endpoint
Failure_Signals: 'Return' button absent on completed order; return form missing line items; reason field missing or not required; no confirmation shown after submission; order detail not updated with return status
Cleanup: none — return request is intentional test action

Title: Return Request - Partial Return
Preconditions: Buyer logged in as {{USER_EMAIL}}; completed order exists with at least 2 line items; return/RMA feature enabled
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/orders; [ACT] click on completed order with 2+ line items; [KEY] note all line items (Item A and Item B); [ASSERT] verify 'Return' or 'Request Return' button visible; [ACT] click 'Return'; [WAIT] wait for return form; [ASSERT] verify both Item A and Item B shown in return form with checkboxes; [ACT] select only Item A (deselect Item B); [ACT] enter return reason; [ACT] submit return request; [WAIT] wait for confirmation; [ASSERT] verify confirmation message shown; [ASSERT] verify return request lists only Item A; [ASSERT] verify Item B NOT included in return request; [NAV] navigate back to order detail; [ASSERT] verify partial return status or badge shown on order
Assertions: [DOM] each line item in return form individually selectable; [STATE] only selected items (Item A) appear in submitted return request; [STATE] unselected item (Item B) absent from return request; [DOM] confirmation message references Item A specifically or shows partial return; [STATE] order detail reflects partial return in progress; [DOM] return form prevents submission with zero items selected
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] POST return mutation payload includes only selected line item IDs; [CONSOLE] no JS errors on partial selection; [NETWORK] 2xx on partial return request
Failure_Signals: return form lacks individual item selection (all-or-nothing only); deselected Item B included in return request anyway; submit allowed with no items selected; order detail not updated after partial return submission

Title: Order Cancellation - Cancel New Order
Preconditions: Buyer logged in as {{USER_EMAIL}}; at least one order exists in 'New' status; cancellation by buyer is enabled for the store
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/orders; [ACT] click on an order with status 'New'; [WAIT] wait for order detail page; [ASSERT] verify 'Cancel Order' button visible on detail page; [ACT] click 'Cancel Order'; [WAIT] wait for cancellation confirmation dialog; [ASSERT] verify confirmation dialog appears with cancel reason field; [ACT] enter cancellation reason (e.g. 'Changed my mind'); [ACT] confirm cancellation in dialog; [WAIT] wait for status update; [ASSERT] verify order status changes to 'Cancelled'; [ASSERT] verify cancellation reason visible on order detail; [NAV] navigate back to /account/orders list; [ASSERT] verify order shows 'Cancelled' status in list
Assertions: [DOM] 'Cancel Order' button present on 'New' status order detail; [DOM] confirmation dialog shown before committing cancellation; [DOM] cancellation reason text field present in dialog; [STATE] order status = 'Cancelled' after confirmation; [DOM] cancellation reason text visible on order detail page; [STATE] order shows 'Cancelled' in /account/orders list
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] POST cancelOrder mutation — response errors[] is empty; [CONSOLE] no errors during cancellation flow; [NETWORK] 2xx on cancel order endpoint
Failure_Signals: 'Cancel Order' button absent on New order; confirmation dialog not shown before cancellation; reason field missing in dialog; status remains 'New' after cancellation confirmed; cancellation reason not stored or not displayed

Title: Order Cancellation - Cancel Button Absent on Shipped Order
Preconditions: Buyer logged in as {{USER_EMAIL}}; at least one order exists in 'Shipped' status
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/orders; [ACT] click on an order with status 'Shipped'; [WAIT] wait for order detail page; [ASSERT] verify 'Cancel Order' button is NOT present on the page; [ASSERT] verify no cancellation option available in any action menu or dropdown; [ASSERT] verify page loads without JS errors; [ASSERT] verify other order actions (e.g. Reorder, view tracking) are still accessible

Title: Order Confirmation Page - Full Details Shown
Preconditions: Buyer logged in as {{USER_EMAIL}}; able to place a new order; payment method and shipping address configured
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to storefront; [ACT] add item to cart; [ACT] proceed through checkout steps; [ACT] place order; [WAIT] wait for order confirmation page to load; [ASSERT] verify order number displayed on confirmation page; [ASSERT] verify ordered items shown with product name and quantity; [ASSERT] verify unit price and line total for each item; [ASSERT] verify order total (subtotal, shipping, taxes, grand total); [ASSERT] verify shipping address shown as entered during checkout; [ASSERT] verify estimated delivery date or shipping method displayed; [ASSERT] verify 'Continue Shopping' or 'View Order' link present
Assertions: [DOM] order number visible on confirmation page (format matches /account/orders order ID); [DOM] all ordered items listed with product name and qty; [MATH] line totals = unit price × qty; [MATH] grand total = subtotal + shipping + taxes; [FORMAT] shipping address matches address entered at checkout; [DOM] shipping method or estimated delivery shown; [DOM] 'Continue Shopping' or 'View Order' action link present and functional; [NAV] 'View Order' link navigates to correct order detail in /account/orders
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] GET order detail by confirmation order ID returns matching data; [CONSOLE] no JS errors on confirmation page; [NETWORK] 2xx on confirmation page load
Failure_Signals: confirmation page shows no order number; ordered items absent from confirmation; totals absent or mathematically incorrect; shipping address missing; no action links on confirmation page
Cleanup: none — order is placed and final

Title: Invoice PDF Download from Order Detail
Preconditions: Buyer logged in as {{USER_EMAIL}}; at least one completed order exists with invoice/PDF download feature enabled for the store
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/orders; [ACT] click on a completed order to open detail; [ASSERT] verify 'Download Invoice' or 'Invoice' button/link visible on order detail page; [ACT] click 'Download Invoice'; [WAIT] wait for download to initiate; [ASSERT] verify file download begins (browser download prompt or file saved); [ASSERT] verify downloaded file has .pdf extension; [ASSERT] verify downloaded file is non-empty (file size > 0 bytes); [ASSERT] verify no error message shown during or after download attempt
Assertions: [DOM] 'Download Invoice' button or link present on completed order detail; [STATE] clicking button triggers file download without navigation away from page; [FORMAT] downloaded file has .pdf extension; [STATE] file size > 0 bytes (non-empty PDF); [DOM] no error toast or error message shown after download

Failure_Signals: 'Download Invoice' button absent on completed order; clicking button shows error message instead of downloading; downloaded file is 0 bytes or corrupted; file extension is not .pdf

Title: Order Search by Order Number
Preconditions: Buyer logged in as {{USER_EMAIL}}; account has multiple orders; at least one order with a known order number exists
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/orders; [KEY] note the order number of any existing order (e.g. ORD-12345); [ASSERT] verify search field or search icon visible on orders list page; [ACT] enter exact order number in search field; [KEY] press Enter or click search button; [WAIT] wait for search results to load; [ASSERT] verify exactly the matching order appears in results; [ASSERT] verify no unrelated orders shown; [ASSERT] verify order number in result matches search input; [ACT] clear the search field; [WAIT] wait for full list to restore; [ASSERT] verify all orders shown again after clearing search

Title: Orders List Pagination
Preconditions: Buyer logged in as {{USER_EMAIL}}; account has more orders than fit on a single page (typically more than 10-20 orders)
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/orders; [ASSERT] verify pagination controls (page numbers or Next/Previous buttons) visible when order count exceeds page size; [KEY] note number of orders shown on page 1; [ACT] click 'Next' or page 2 button; [WAIT] wait for page 2 to load; [ASSERT] verify different orders shown on page 2 (no overlap with page 1); [ASSERT] verify current page indicator updated (e.g. page 2 highlighted); [ACT] click 'Previous' or page 1 button; [WAIT] wait for page 1 to reload; [ASSERT] verify same orders from page 1 shown again; [ASSERT] verify page indicator returns to page 1
Assertions: [DOM] pagination controls visible when total order count exceeds page size threshold; [STATE] page 2 content differs from page 1 — no duplicate order rows; [DOM] current page indicator reflects active page; [STATE] navigating back to page 1 restores original order list; [MATH] total order count across all pages = total orders in account; [DOM] page size consistent between pages
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] paginated orders query with page/offset params returns distinct result sets per page; [CONSOLE] no errors on pagination navigation; [NETWORK] 2xx on each page request
Failure_Signals: pagination controls absent when orders exceed page size; page 2 shows same orders as page 1; current page indicator not updated; back navigation reloads all orders ignoring pagination

Title: Order Date Range Filter

Steps: [NAV] Navigate to /account/orders; [ASSERT] verify date range filter control (date pickers or date range input) visible on orders list page; [ACT] set start date to first day of previous calendar month; [ACT] set end date to last day of previous calendar month; [ACT] apply or submit the date filter; [WAIT] wait for filtered results; [ASSERT] verify all displayed orders have order dates within the selected range; [ASSERT] verify orders from the current month or older months outside range are absent; [ACT] clear date range filter; [WAIT] wait for full list to reload; [ASSERT] verify all orders restored
Assertions: [DOM] date range filter control present on /account/orders page; [STATE] all visible orders have order dates within the selected start and end date (inclusive); [STATE] orders outside the date range absent from filtered results; [FORMAT] order dates in results formatted consistently and fall within selected range; [STATE] clearing date range filter restores unfiltered order list; [DOM] active filter state visually indicated while filter is applied
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] orders query with date range params returns only in-range records; [CONSOLE] no errors on date filter application; [NETWORK] 2xx on filtered request
Failure_Signals: date range filter absent on orders page; filter shows orders outside the specified range; clearing filter does not restore full list; date picker interaction causes JS errors

Title: Order Status - Processing State Reflected on Storefront
Preconditions: Order exists in 'New' status placed by {{USER_EMAIL}}; admin credentials available at {{ADMIN_URL}}; admin can access Orders module
Steps: [ADMIN] Log in to Admin SPA at {{ADMIN_URL}}; [NAV] navigate to Orders module; [ACT] find target order by order number; [ACT] change order status from 'New' to 'Processing'; [ACT] save the status change; [ASSERT] verify admin save succeeds without error; [NAV] switch to storefront at {{FRONT_URL}} as {{USER_EMAIL}}; [NAV] navigate to /account/orders; [ASSERT] find the target order in list; [ASSERT] verify order list shows 'Processing' status for that order; [ACT] click order to open detail; [ASSERT] verify order detail page shows status = 'Processing'; [ASSERT] verify status history or timeline shows transition from New to Processing
Assertions: [ADMIN] admin status save completes without error message; [STATE] storefront order list shows 'Processing' status after admin update; [STATE] order detail page status = 'Processing' (not 'New'); [DOM] status indicator visually distinct from 'New' status (different color or label); [DOM] status history or audit trail (if present) shows New → Processing transition
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ADMIN] verify admin Orders list reflects Processing status; [API] GET order detail returns status=Processing; [CONSOLE] no errors on storefront order detail after admin status change
Failure_Signals: admin save fails silently; storefront still shows 'New' after admin sets Processing; order detail shows incorrect intermediate status; status history absent or shows incorrect transition
Cleanup: none — status update is intentional test action

Title: RFQ Form - Buyer Modifies Item Quantities Before Submission
Preconditions: Buyer logged in as {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}; cart contains at least 2 items with initial quantities set (e.g. Item A qty 2, Item B qty 3); RFQ feature enabled for the org
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to cart page; [KEY] note Item A qty = 2 and Item B qty = 3; [ACT] click 'Request Quote' button; [WAIT] wait for RFQ form to load; [ASSERT] verify RFQ form pre-populates Item A qty = 2 and Item B qty = 3; [ACT] change Item A quantity on the RFQ form to 5; [ASSERT] verify quantity field accepts the new value 5; [ASSERT] verify Item B quantity remains unchanged at 3; [ACT] fill specifications/notes field; [ACT] click 'Submit' on RFQ form; [WAIT] wait for confirmation; [NAV] navigate to /account/quotes; [ACT] click on the newly created quote to open detail; [ASSERT] verify quote detail shows Item A qty = 5; [ASSERT] verify quote detail shows Item B qty = 3; [ASSERT] verify cart still shows original quantities (Item A qty 2, Item B qty 3) unchanged
Assertions: [DOM] RFQ form shows editable quantity fields per line item; [DOM] quantity field accepts the modified value 5 for Item A; [STATE] RFQ form quantity for Item B unchanged at 3 after editing Item A; [STATE] submitted quote shows Item A qty = 5 (modified value) not 2 (cart value); [STATE] submitted quote shows Item B qty = 3 (original value); [STATE] cart quantities unaffected by RFQ quantity edits (cart Item A remains 2)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] createQuoteFromCart mutation payload includes modified qty 5 for Item A; [API] quote detail response shows qty=5 for Item A; [CONSOLE] no errors on RFQ form quantity edit; [NETWORK] 2xx on quote submission
Failure_Signals: RFQ form quantity fields read-only (cannot be edited); edited quantity reverts to cart quantity on submission; quote detail shows cart quantity (2) not RFQ quantity (5) for Item A; cart quantity altered when RFQ quantity changed
Cleanup: reject quote via admin if accepted/pending
